# "Navigating by the Morning Star"
## Scripture
"For we did not follow cleverly devised myths when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but we were eyewitnesses of his majesty. For when he received honor and glory from God the Father, and the voice was borne to him by the Majestic Glory, 'This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased,' we ourselves heard this very voice borne from heaven, for we were with him on the holy mountain. And we have something more sure, the prophetic word, to which you will do well to pay attention as to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts" - 2 Peter 1:16-19.

## Reflection
As we walk through the journey of faith, we often face uncertain times and dark places. But just as the morning star shines brightly in the darkness, guiding sailors and travelers, Jesus Christ is our guiding light, illuminating our path and giving us direction. The morning star represents the hope and promise of a new day, and in Christ, we have the assurance of a brighter tomorrow. As we navigate the challenges of life, let us fix our eyes on the morning star, Jesus, and trust in His power and presence to guide us through.

## Prayer
Dear Heavenly Father, thank you for the gift of Jesus, our morning star, who shines brightly in the darkness. Help us to trust in His power and presence, and to navigate the challenges of life with confidence and hope. May Your Word be a lamp to our feet, guiding us until the day dawns and the morning star rises in our hearts. Amen.
